channel.title: Entuned Blog — Retail Music Strategy
channel.description: Research-backed insights on retail music strategy, in-store customer behavior, and AI-powered audio optimization.

item.title: Mood Media Alternatives in 2026: What Retail Leaders Are Switching To
item.description: Retailers evaluating Mood Media alternatives in 2026 have more options than ever. Here's how the major players compare — and what's changed with AI.

item.title: $2 Billion in Sensors. No Real-Time Levers.
item.description: The global in-store analytics market is approaching $2 billion. It still can't tell a retailer what to change in real time. Audio is the missing intervention layer.

item.title: What Happens After Ninety Days
item.description: The first week is setup. The first month is noise. By day ninety, the data starts telling you how music matters in your specific store.

item.title: AI-Generated Music for Retail: What's Real and What's Hype | Entuned
item.description: AI music generation is real and improving fast. Here is what it can actually do for retail audio today, and where the hype outpaces the technology.

item.title: AI vs. Traditional In-Store Music: What's Changed
item.description: How AI-powered retail music compares to traditional providers on targeting, measurement, and licensing.

item.title: The Battery Ventures Thesis, Extended
item.description: Battery Ventures backed RetailNext to build the complete physical retail data layer. The next acquisition in that thesis is probably audio.

item.title: The CFO's Case for Retail Audio | Entuned
item.description: Retail audio affects four measurable numbers: dwell time, basket size, willingness to pay, and return visits. The financial case is cleaner than expected.

item.title: Closing the Loop on Retail Analytics
item.description: Retail analytics can tell you exactly what's happening in your store. But it can't do anything about it in real time. Audio is the missing action layer.

item.title: Your Employees Hear It 2,000 Hours a Year
item.description: Your customer is in the store for twenty minutes. Your staff is in it for eight hours. The music that works for both is the same music.

item.title: Every Store Teaches the Next One
item.description: How Entuned's fleet learning model turns every retail deployment into a data asset — correlating musical variables with sales outcomes across locations.

item.title: How Specialty Wine Retailers Use Music to Sell More Expensive Bottles
item.description: The Areni and Kim study showed classical music moved wine buyers toward premium bottles. The mechanism is quality inference, and it applies beyond wine.

item.title: Why Longer Visits Don't Automatically Mean Bigger Receipts
item.description: Dwell time and spending are not connected by a straight line. The gap between stayed longer and spent more is where most retail music strategies fail.

item.title: Luxury Priming Is Real — And You Don't Have to Be a Luxury Brand to Use It
item.description: Refined music primes quality perception and raises willingness to pay in any retail environment. Luxury priming does not require a luxury brand.

item.title: Major Key Does Not Mean Happy Customer.
item.description: Positive valence and commercial effectiveness are not the same thing. Mild negative valence is among the most commercially interesting states in retail.

item.title: How to Measure the ROI of In-Store Music
item.description: Most retailers cannot measure music's impact on sales because their music systems provide no data. Here's what measurement actually requires.

item.title: The Most Famous Study in Retail Music Is Wrong. Sort Of.
item.description: Milliman's 38% sales lift is the most cited retail music finding. The dwell time effect replicates. The sales effect does not — and the reason matters.

item.title: Your Music Provider Doesn't Know What RetailNext Knows
item.description: Your analytics platform and your music provider share the same store and have never exchanged a single data point. That gap is costing you.

item.title: Music Was Never Made For Your Store
item.description: Every track in your licensing catalog was created for something other than your store. That mismatch has consequences most retailers have never examined.

item.title: Why the Next Big Retail Tech Acquisition Will Be in Audio
item.description: Retail tech acquisitions follow a pattern. Audio is the next surface — and the window is open now.

item.title: Psychographic Profiling for Retail: Beyond Demographics | Entuned
item.description: Demographics tell you who your customer is. Psychographics explain why they buy — and how psychographic data translates into retail music decisions.
